How to Add a New Recipe
========================

A ``Recipe`` class is the thin shell that ``pyesorex`` sees.  It holds
metadata and a pointer to the ``RecipeImpl`` class that contains the actual
algorithm.

Most of the work is in the ``RecipeImpl``; see :doc:`add_algorithm` for that
part.  This guide covers the ``Recipe`` wrapper.

Step 1: Define the Recipe class
---------------------------------

In the same file as your ``RecipeImpl``, add a ``Recipe`` subclass:

.. code-block:: python

    from pymetis.engine.recipes.recipe import Recipe
    from pymetis.engine.core.parameter import ParameterList, ParameterEnum

    from .metis_lm_measure_background_impl import MetisLmMeasureBackgroundImpl


    class MetisLmMeasureBackground(Recipe):

        # --- Mandatory pyesorex attributes ---
        _name        = "metis_lm_measure_background"
        _version     = "0.1"
        _author      = "Your Name"
        _email       = "your.email@example.com"
        _synopsis    = "Measure background level in LM-band science frames"
        _description = (
            "Measures the background level in a set of LM-band science frames "
            "after subtracting a master dark."
        )

        # --- DRLD traceability (used by the autorecipe Sphinx extension) ---
        _matched_keywords: set[str] = {'ESO DPR TYPE', 'ESO DET DIT'}
        _algorithm = """
            - Load raw LM-band science frames
            - Subtract master dark from each frame
            - Median-combine the dark-subtracted frames
            - Compute mean and RMS of the combined background map
        """
        _requirements: set[str] = set()    # e.g. {"METIS-7001"}
        _templates:    set[str] = set()    # e.g. {"METIS_img_lm_obs_AutoChopNod"}

        # --- Recipe parameters ---
        parameters = ParameterList([
            ParameterEnum(
                name=f"{_name}.stacking.method",
                context=_name,
                description="Method used to combine frames",
                default="median",
                alternatives=("average", "median", "sigclip"),
            ),
        ])

        # --- Pointer to the implementation class ---
        Impl = MetisLmMeasureBackgroundImpl

Mandatory attributes
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

.. list-table::
   :header-rows: 1
   :widths: 20 80

   * - Attribute
     - Notes
   * - ``_name``
     - Lowercase, underscore-separated.  This is the string users pass to
       ``pyesorex`` and to EDPS ``-t`` flags.
   * - ``_version``
     - Semantic version string, e.g. ``"0.1"``.
   * - ``_author``, ``_email``
     - Contact for this specific recipe.
   * - ``_synopsis``
     - Single line; shown by ``pyesorex --recipes``.
   * - ``_description``
     - Longer text; shown by ``pyesorex --man-page``.
   * - ``Impl``
     - Must point to the corresponding ``RecipeImpl`` subclass.
   * - ``parameters``
     - ``ParameterList`` (may be empty: ``ParameterList([])``).

DRLD attributes
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

.. list-table::
   :header-rows: 1
   :widths: 20 80

   * - Attribute
     - Notes
   * - ``_matched_keywords``
     - Set of FITS keywords used to group/match input frames.
   * - ``_algorithm``
     - Free-text description of the reduction steps.  Appears in the man page
       and in the DRLD reference page generated by ``autorecipe``.
   * - ``_requirements``
     - Set of requirement IDs (e.g. ``{"METIS-5997"}``).  Used only for DRLD
       traceability; not interpreted at runtime.
   * - ``_templates``
     - Set of OB template names that trigger this recipe in normal operation.

Step 2: Create a pyrecipes wrapper
------------------------------------

``pyesorex`` looks for recipe classes in the ``PYESOREX_PLUGIN_DIR``.  The METIS
pipeline uses a thin wrapper file in ``metisp/pyrecipes/`` that imports the
Recipe class::

    # metisp/pyrecipes/metis_lm_measure_background.py
    from pymetis.instruments.metis.recipes.lm_img.metis_lm_measure_background \
        import MetisLmMeasureBackground

This ensures ``pyesorex`` discovers the class when it scans the plugin directory.

Step 3: Add to the reference docs
-----------------------------------

Create a new RST file under ``docs/reference/imaging/`` (or the appropriate
sub-directory) and add the ``autorecipe`` directive::

    .. autorecipe:: pymetis.instruments.metis.recipes.lm_img.metis_lm_measure_background.MetisLmMeasureBackground

Then add the new file to the ``docs/reference/imaging/index.rst`` toctree.

Step 4: Verify
---------------

.. code-block:: bash

    # Check pyesorex discovers the new recipe
    pyesorex --recipes | grep metis_lm_measure_background

    # View the auto-generated man page
    pyesorex --man-page metis_lm_measure_background
